data_coverage_summary: Lightspeed publicly organizes developer access across retail, eCom, restaurant, and golf APIs, with retail-specific documentation for X-Series and R-Series commerce platforms.
buyer_guidance: Use Lightspeed Retail APIs for retail and point-of-sale integrations across Lightspeed X-Series and R-Series environments, with adjacent connectivity into eCom and other Lightspeed product families. This is a commerce platform, not an asset or telemetry platform.
overview: Lightspeed's public developer portal organizes the platform into eCom APIs, Retail APIs, Restaurant APIs, and Golf API, with Retail APIs specifically pointing developers to X-Series and R-Series documentation. This public structure confirms a multi-product commerce platform with write-capable retail integration surfaces for POS and retail workflows, plus adjacent commerce connectivity to eCom channels. The publicly visible portal is category-level rather than entity-level, so this row reflects platform-level retail integration capability rather than object-specific schema coverage. Asset data is Unsupported. Telemetry data is Unsupported. Writeback is Supported.

available_data.description: Lightspeed publicly exposes retail-specific APIs across major product series.
The portal also shows how retail APIs sit within a larger Lightspeed commerce estate.
data_points: Restaurant and golf product family integrations
Unified developer portal across product lines
Regional or series-specific documentation entry points

ingest_considerations: Lightspeed's public developer home page is a portal into several product-series docs, so exact retail objects and workflows should be refined against X-Series or R-Series references when one specific platform matters.
This row should remain scoped to retail APIs even though the public portal also shows restaurant, eCom, and golf APIs.
No exact public machine-readable spec artifact was confirmed for the aggregate Lightspeed Retail row during this pass.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: Lightspeed's public developer portal groups APIs into eCom, Retail, Restaurant, and Golf categories and explicitly points Retail APIs to X-Series and R-Series documentation, confirming public retail integration surfaces within the Lightspeed commerce platform.
